instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertions on Should you Buy a Critical Illness or a Disease-Specific Plan - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: critical illness cover, critical illness insurance, critical illness plan
Body Content:
Introduction
Everyone today knows somebody suffering from a serious non-communicable disease (NCD). More than 50% of the deaths in India are caused due to NCDs, which include cancer, heart ailments, serious kidney failure, etc.
While previously it was believed that NCDs only affect those who are old or genetically predisposed to contract these diseases, the world has changed and so have people’s lifestyles, which are putting nearly everyone at risk of contracting NCDs.
However, as the world has evolved to see rising incidences of NCDs, the solutions have also evolved. Aside from regular health insurance plans, which cover the major costs of hospitalisation, there are several other kinds of health insurance policies that can help policyholders battle their illness without having to dip into their savings or other investments.
Critical Illness Plans
Critical illness cover is one of the most common kinds of insurance policies that have come up to deal with the increased incidences of NCDs. They offer several benefits over the typical health insurance policy since the latter usually only covers costs related to hospitalisation. Read on below to learn the benefits of a critical illness insurance policy.
Many critical diseases are covered under this plan, sometimes ranging till 30. With the wider array of illnesses being covered, consumers are assured of more value for the money they pay as premiums.
The critical illness plan offers fixed benefits, which amounts to the policyholder receiving a lump sum amount when a disease covered by the plan is diagnosed. Owing to this, a range of expenses can be covered, including travelling for treatment and even the expenses incurred in booking hotel rooms near the place of treatment.
The critical illness cover is also beneficial for compensating the policyholder for any loss of pay they may incur due to their illness and its treatment.
Standalone Disease Plan
In recent times, many people are also opting for standalone disease plans. While they cover lesser scope than a comprehensive critical illness insurance policy, it is necessary to understand the benefits they have to offer before you can make a choice between the two. Read below to understand the benefits of such a plan.
There are some diseases that are seeing higher incidence among the Indian population, even within the NCD category. These include heart ailments and cancer. Additionally, the chances of a person contracting these diseases are higher if they already have a genetic predisposition towards the disease due to others in their family also suffering from it. For such individuals, standalone disease plans that cover that specific NCD could be beneficial.
Since standalone disease plans only offer coverage for one particular disease, their premiums are cheaper than premiums for critical illness plans. They are also extremely easy to avail since you can simply bundle it in with your existing health insurance policy conveniently.
Standalone disease plans offer a part of the lump sum payment of the sum assured in the early stages of the disease being diagnosed.
It is also important to remember that the treatment for all diseases varies. For instance, the treatment of cancer has many stages and can go on for much longer and typically involves higher costs. Thus, by opting for a standalone disease plan, a person with a genetic predisposition to cancer can rest assured of receiving a higher sum assured by paying lesser premiums than they would have to with a critical illness cover.
Conclusion
As is evident from the information provided above, there are several benefits to both standalone disease plans and critical illness insurance policies. Depending on their situation, it is best for an individual to decide on which policy might be best suited for their needs. While a standalone plan does guarantee a higher sum assured for treatment of a specific disease, it is also important to note that it is extremely difficult to predict which disease a person is likely to contract. Though genetic predisposition to a disease can increase a person’s chances of contracting it, there is no guarantee that they will contract it or that they will not contract a different NCD. Thus, it might be better to opt for a critical illness cover that covers a higher number of diseases and ensures that all treatment costs as well as any loss of income arising from the disease are covered as well.

(Context: SEO Insertions on Should you Buy a Critical Illness or a Disease-Specific Plan -...)
